jokes.text: Don't think this guy's gonna make it. You hear me? Did you have an OPA. Breathing's irregular and weak.
jokes.setup: Characters speaking with medical authority about a patient
jokes.punchline: They're clearly incompetent but using medical jargon incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny setup - characters pretending competence they don't have. The medical jargon misuse is character-specific incompetence.

dialogue.line: Don't think this guy's gonna make it. You hear me? Did you have an OPA. Breathing's irregular and weak.

jokes.text: We don't know this guy from Adam. We've never seen him before at all. He's a total outsider. I never saw him before in the bar at all.
jokes.setup: Characters trying to distance themselves from responsibility
jokes.punchline: Escalating overemphasis reveals their obvious guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - the more they protest innocence, the guiltier they sound. Classic character behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We don't know this guy from Adam. We've never seen him before at all. He's a total outsider.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I never saw him before in the bar at all.

jokes.text: I thought he was, like, a jazzman. I mean, he had a ponytail. He had a piano-key tie.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why they gave stranger hot pepper
jokes.punchline: Racist/classist stereotyping about jazz musicians and spice tolerance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic chain - visual appearance → jazz musician → spice tolerance. Perfectly captures character's warped reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I thought he was, like, a jazzman. I mean, he had a ponytail. He had a piano-key tie.

jokes.text: His goddamn heart just stopped for no reason. Not no reason. You gave him a hot pepper. Don't... They don't have to know that.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing victim's condition with EMTs present
jokes.punchline: Mac accidentally revealing the cause while Dennis tries to cover it up
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their incompetence at covering up their crime

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: His goddamn heart just stopped for no reason.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Not no reason. You gave him a hot pepper.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't... They don't have to know that.

jokes.text: You'd think that a jazzman would be more accustomed to spices, you know? Right? You'd think he'd be fine.
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the ridiculous logic as if it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that escalates the absurdity by having Dennis validate Mac's insane reasoning.

dialogue.line: You'd think that a jazzman would be more accustomed to spices, you know? Right? You'd think he'd be fine.

jokes.text: Sorry. Wh-What's with the food delivery here? Oh. I'm, uh, doing a Postmates thing as a side gig.
jokes.setup: Someone questioning food delivery in a bar
jokes.punchline: Dee doing gig work despite owning part of the bar
jokes.explanation: Character-specific absurdity - Dee's desperation driving her to do delivery despite bar ownership.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Sorry. Wh-What's with the food delivery here?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh. I'm, uh, doing a Postmates thing as a side gig.

jokes.text: You don't think I can make a burger and smash it? I'll smash a burger for you.
jokes.setup: Frank ordering food instead of eating Charlie's cooking
jokes.punchline: Charlie's literal interpretation of 'smash burger'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his literal interpretation of culinary terms reveals his fundamental misunderstanding of cooking.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You don't think I can make a burger and smash it? I'll smash a burger for you.

jokes.text: What the hell, Frank? A 5% tip? Pedal faster. It's cold as shit.
jokes.punchline: Frank's cheapness and cruel dismissal of Dee's effort
jokes.explanation: Frank's characteristic cruelty and cheapness perfectly displayed in the tip and dismissive command.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What the hell, Frank? A 5% tip?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Pedal faster. It's cold as shit.

jokes.text: Hey, did anybody else get, like, a crazy rush from that experience? I did. It's nice to have a little action around here, right?
jokes.punchline: They're excited by someone's near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - their narcissistic inability to feel empathy turns trauma into entertainment for them.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey, did anybody else get, like, a crazy rush from that experience? I did. It's nice to have a little action around here, right?

jokes.text: You know, it's as close to playing God as you can get, you know? And the lifestyle. There's an appeal to it for sure.
jokes.punchline: Mac's grandiose delusions about emergency medical work
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency to romanticize and aggrandize everything, turning basic medical assistance into godlike power.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know, it's as close to playing God as you can get, you know? And the lifestyle. There's an appeal to it for sure.

jokes.text: You know that they bang the doctors and the nurses, right? Of course they do. Of course they do. Are you kidding me?
jokes.punchline: Reducing career choice to sexual opportunities
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - their shallow, sex-obsessed view of career choices reveals their priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know that they bang the doctors and the nurses, right?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Of course they do. Of course they do. Are you kidding me?

jokes.text: The lifestyle is spicier than one of Frank's ghost peppers.
jokes.punchline: Connecting lifestyle excitement to the ghost pepper incident
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that connects to pepper theme but lacks impact. Functional but uninspired wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The lifestyle is spicier than one of Frank's ghost peppers.

jokes.text: You're not cake, are you? Nah. It's just a joke between me and the guys...
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about cake followed by unclear explanation
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Frank moment. The complete randomness of the cake question is perfectly inexplicable Frank behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're not cake, are you?

dialogue.speaker: Jazzman
dialogue.line: Nah. It's just a joke between me and the guys...

jokes.text: Well, y-you know, actually, it's not my fault. Well, sure, I gave you a hot pepper and you had a heart attack, but, you know, I-I kind of attribute that to the... you know, the jazzman lifestyle more than the pepper.
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflects blame back to victim's 'jazzman lifestyle'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic - admits guilt then immediately blames victim. Great callback to jazzman stereotype escalating the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Well, y-you know, actually, it's not my fault. Well, sure, I gave you a hot pepper and you had a heart attack, but, you know, I-I kind of attribute that to the... you know, the jazzman lifestyle more than the pepper.

jokes.text: Eh? Just checking about the cake thing. You never know with those guys.
jokes.punchline: Mysterious reference to 'those guys' and cake suspicions
jokes.explanation: Mysterious and quotable line that adds to the absurd cake conspiracy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Eh? Just checking about the cake thing. You never know with those guys.

jokes.text: The Lawyer. What's with the getup? It's not a getup. It's an eye patch from when I was attacked by a bird.
jokes.punchline: Bird attack injury from previous episode/incident
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy from previous episode consequences. The matter-of-fact delivery of absurd injury explanation works well.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What's with the getup?

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: It's not a getup. It's an eye patch from when I was attacked by a bird.

jokes.text: Gruesome. I wish I'd have seen that. You did see it. I don't remember.
jokes.punchline: Frank's complete lack of empathy and memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank - expressing sadistic curiosity about something he witnessed and forgot. Great economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: Gruesome.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I wish I'd have seen that.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: You did see it.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I don't remember.

jokes.text: You don't remember being present in a court of law while a wild animal pecked out someone's eye? Can you be more specific?
jokes.setup: Lawyer's incredulity at Frank's memory lapse
jokes.punchline: Frank asking for more specificity about the bizarre incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the more specific and bizarre the description, the more Frank treats it as insufficiently detailed. Perfect character obtuseness.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: You don't remember being present in a court of law while a wild animal pecked out someone's eye?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Can you be more specific?

jokes.text: I'm innocent. You literally just admitted it. I was lying. To a man in a coma? I thought he was cake.
jokes.setup: Frank denying guilt after admitting it
jokes.punchline: Escalating denials ending with cake callback
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Frank's lies becoming more absurd. The cake callback as an excuse is brilliantly nonsensical.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm innocent.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: You literally just admitted it.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I was lying.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: To a man in a coma?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I thought he was cake.

jokes.text: Well, instead of taking 100% of the dose 10% of the time, we take 10% of the dose 100% of the time. That is scientifically more better.
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation ending with 'more better'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pseudo-intellectual persona perfectly undermined by 'more better.' Great structure building to grammatical failure.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, instead of taking 100% of the dose 10% of the time, we take 10% of the dose 100% of the time. That is scientifically more better.

jokes.text: The snugness, uh, really accentuates your physique. If-If that's okay to say. Uh... It's not, but let's continue, huh?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to compliment EMT instructor
jokes.punchline: Immediate shutdown of his inappropriate comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis inappropriate behavior immediately shut down. Good cringe comedy structure.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The snugness, uh, really accentuates your physique. If-If that's okay to say. Uh... It's not, but let's continue, huh?

jokes.text: When I just referred to the gentleman to my right as my partner, I meant in medicine, not in life. In life, I am very much single.
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily emphasizing his availability to instructor
jokes.explanation: Dennis's desperation to hit on instructor despite professional setting. Character-appropriate behavior but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: When I just referred to the gentleman to my right as my partner, I meant in medicine, not in life. In life, I am very much single.

jokes.text: Now, when we get to the chest compressions, should I take your shirt off or should you take mine off? No shirts come off.
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately sexualizing medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Predictable Dennis behavior but effective cringe moment. Character-appropriate sexual inappropriateness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now, when we get to the chest compressions, should I take your shirt off or should you take mine off?

dialogue.speaker: EMT Instructor
dialogue.line: No shirts come off.

jokes.text: Well, somebody doesn't understand double time. You see, I'm pushing to a much spicier song. Is everyone familiar with the Gipsy Kings's "Bamboléo"?
jokes.setup: Mac being corrected on CPR rhythm
jokes.punchline: Defending wrong technique with different musical reference and spice callback
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Mac moment - combining his musical pretensions with the spice theme while being completely wrong about CPR. Great quotable line.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, somebody doesn't understand double time. You see, I'm pushing to a much spicier song. Is everyone familiar with the Gipsy Kings's "Bamboléo"?

jokes.text: Say, "Yes, Chef." Why? Because the stakes are, uh, very high, you know, this is high-stakes stuff. Stakes of what? The cooking! It's-It's life-and-death stuff, man.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking Frank to say 'Yes, Chef'
jokes.punchline: Escalating the importance of basic cooking to life-and-death stakes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie escalation - taking cooking way too seriously. The repeated 'stakes' creates good wordplay momentum.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Say, "Yes, Chef." Why? Because the stakes are, uh, very high, you know, this is high-stakes stuff. Stakes of what? The cooking! It's-It's life-and-death stuff, man.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a brick-based medium. Look at that. Yeah, that's my favorite part.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about Charlie's burger-making technique
jokes.punchline: Charlie using literal brick to smash burger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Charlie's literal interpretation of 'smash burger.' Visual gag with perfect character logic. 'Brick-based medium' is wonderfully absurd terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's a brick-based medium. Look at that. Yeah, that's my favorite part.

jokes.text: That's because I got smashed by a Prius. Whoops! Yeah. I need some shin guards, I think.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning car accident with pun on 'smashed'
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup with pun on smash theme. Dee's casual attitude toward injury is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That's because I got smashed by a Prius. Whoops! Yeah. I need some shin guards, I think.

jokes.text: You gotta say, "Yes, chef," "No, chef," "On your left, chef," "Corner, chef." These kind of things. It's gotta work that way, Dee.
jokes.setup: Charlie establishing kitchen hierarchy with Dee
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top professional kitchen terminology for basic food delivery
jokes.explanation: Charlie's obsession with professional kitchen culture escalating. Good callback to his earlier chef demands.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You gotta say, "Yes, chef," "No, chef," "On your left, chef," "Corner, chef." These kind of things. It's gotta work that way, Dee.

jokes.text: The illegality of what we're doing is spicy enough to get me off.
jokes.setup: Mac describing their illegal EMT activities
jokes.punchline: Sexual arousal from breaking the law
jokes.explanation: Mac's sexual excitement about illegal activity with spice callback. Economical and character-specific dark humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: The illegality of what we're doing is spicy enough to get me off.

jokes.text: This is Flaming Pepper, new truck on the rotation. We're only about a block away. We're gonna stop by.
jokes.punchline: Mac using 'Flaming Pepper' as their fake EMT call sign
jokes.explanation: Good callback to pepper theme with absurd professional call sign. Shows their commitment to the pepper lifestyle.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is Flaming Pepper, new truck on the rotation. We're only about a block away. We're gonna stop by.

jokes.text: Now you're not microdosing anymore. You've slipped into macrodosing. Macrodosing. That's cool 'cause it's my name.
jokes.setup: Dennis scolding Mac about pepper consumption
jokes.punchline: Mac pleased that overdosing shares his name
jokes.explanation: Good pun callback to microdosing concept. Mac's narcissistic pleasure at name connection is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Now you're not microdosing anymore. You've slipped into macrodosing.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Macrodosing. That's cool 'cause it's my name.

jokes.text: Ma'am, you're gonna... you're gonna wanna keep that knee lukewarm. You're gonna wanna take short, shallow breaths.
jokes.setup: Giving medical advice to injured woman
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong and contradictory medical advice
jokes.explanation: Their complete medical incompetence on display. 'Lukewarm' and 'shallow breaths' are hilariously wrong advice.

dialogue.line: Ma'am, you're gonna... you're gonna wanna keep that knee lukewarm. You're gonna wanna take short, shallow breaths.

jokes.text: Oh, my eye is burning. Was there something on your thumb? It's probably unrelated.
jokes.setup: Patient experiencing new symptoms during examination
jokes.punchline: Mac dismissing obvious connection to pepper residue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their incompetence causing new injuries. Mac's casual dismissal is perfectly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: Oh, my eye is burning.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Was there something on your thumb? It's probably unrelated.

jokes.text: You wanna pop that sweater off? What? No, I need to rinse my eye. Well, the sweater needs to come off.
jokes.punchline: Dennis prioritizing getting her shirt off over medical need
jokes.explanation: Dennis's sexual priorities overriding medical emergency. Predictable but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You wanna pop that sweater off?

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: What? No, I need to rinse my eye.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, the sweater needs to come off.

jokes.text: Yeah, let's make her unconscious. Why? I feel like this is becoming a whole thing.
jokes.punchline: Mac suggesting unconsciousness to avoid dealing with problems
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - Mac's casual suggestion to knock out patient to avoid inconvenience. Perfect economy and character darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, let's make her unconscious.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I feel like this is becoming a whole thing.

jokes.text: She definitely wants to bang me, dude. Yeah, I think so.
jokes.setup: Patient they just injured and mistreated
jokes.punchline: Complete delusion about her romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Their complete disconnect from reality. Sexual delusion despite obviously traumatizing the patient.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She definitely wants to bang me, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, I think so.

jokes.text: Spice pot is spicy but needs more peppers. Peppers coming up. More peppers. I needed a "Yes, chef" there.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee in kitchen chaos
jokes.punchline: Charlie demanding proper kitchen etiquette during crisis
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Charlie's chef obsession continuing during chaos. Character priorities remaining warped under pressure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Spice pot is spicy but needs more peppers. Peppers coming up. More peppers. I needed a "Yes, chef" there.

jokes.text: I don't think he's gonna make it. Looks like he's gonna die. Should we pepper? You want some pepper, sir?
jokes.punchline: Their solution is to offer him the thing that's been causing problems
jokes.explanation: Dark callback - their pepper obsession overriding medical logic. Offering poison to dying person perfectly captures their dysfunction.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't think he's gonna make it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Looks like he's gonna die.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Should we pepper?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You want some pepper, sir?

jokes.text: Anything for my first responders. My guy. My guy. My guy. Getting high on your own supply over there?
jokes.setup: Charlie giving Mac and Dennis peppers
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating Charlie's cooking like drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Good drug dealing parallel with pepper addiction. Charlie's pride in supplying their habit works well.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Anything for my first responders. My guy. My guy. My guy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Getting high on your own supply over there?

jokes.text: A lot of blood in my line of work too. Well, there shouldn't be. Yeah, but you get the finger. I got Frank once or twice.
jokes.setup: Comparing their jobs to EMT work
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually mentioning cutting Frank while cooking
jokes.explanation: Dark casual revelation about Charlie's dangerous cooking practices. The matter-of-fact delivery enhances the horror.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: A lot of blood in my line of work too.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, there shouldn't be.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you get the finger.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I got Frank once or twice.

jokes.text: I'm sensing an opportunity to smash all of these ideas together... and really spice up our lives. Now I'm gonna float out something that I believe to be... a very reasonable proposition.
jokes.setup: Mac having a realization about combining their schemes
jokes.punchline: Callback to Dennis's earlier identical speech pattern and phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Mac copying Dennis's speech patterns and delusions

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm sensing an opportunity to smash all of these ideas together... and really spice up our lives. Now I'm gonna float out something that I believe to be... a very reasonable proposition.

jokes.text: Popcorn ceiling, right next to the highway. This place is trash.
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting the professional space
jokes.explanation: Frank's characteristic rudeness and materialism on display. Economical insult but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Popcorn ceiling, right next to the highway. This place is trash.

jokes.text: Oh. Guess what, bozo? I don't care whether you eat it or not, but if you don't tip me, I'm taking out the other eye.
jokes.setup: Lawyer refusing to tip for food delivery
jokes.punchline: Dee threatening to remove his remaining eye
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark threat with perfect callback to his existing injury

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh. Guess what, bozo? I don't care whether you eat it or not, but if you don't tip me, I'm taking out the other eye.

jokes.text: Snake bite. That's kind of exciting. Whoa! That's very interesting to me. I've always wanted to suck out snake venom, you know this.
jokes.punchline: Mac's inappropriate excitement about oral venom extraction
jokes.explanation: Mac's sexual obsession making him excited about medical procedure. Character-appropriate but predictable innuendo.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Snake bite. That's kind of exciting. Whoa! That's very interesting to me. I've always wanted to suck out snake venom, you know this.

jokes.text: Got a possible code W59 in progress at Snyder and Pattison. Pepper? Pattison? That's like right here. Pepper? Pepper, anyone? Oh! W59? What is that? Snake bite.
jokes.punchline: Mac mishearing 'Pattison' as 'Pepper' while high on peppers
jokes.explanation: Good drug-induced mishearing that connects to the pepper theme

dialogue.line: Got a possible code W59 in progress at Snyder and Pattison.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Pepper?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Pattison?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's like right here. Pepper? Pepper, anyone?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh! W59? What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Snake bite.

jokes.text: What's in that? That is 100% juiced Carolina Reaper tea. I got him. Got him. Reaper tea.
jokes.punchline: Frank's successful poisoning with extreme pepper extract
jokes.explanation: Frank's revenge plan succeeding with maximum pepper dosage. His gleeful celebration of poisoning someone is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: The Lawyer
dialogue.line: What's in that?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That is 100% juiced Carolina Reaper tea.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I got him. Got him. Reaper tea.

jokes.text: Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo.
jokes.punchline: Mac using wrong rhythm from earlier while performing actual CPR
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the CPR class moment. Mac's commitment to the wrong technique in actual emergency perfectly captures his stubborn incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo. Bamboléo.

jokes.text: Goddamn it, Dennis. You have to yell "corner!" I can't see. What? Goddamn it! I think I overdosed on the peppers.
jokes.punchline: Charlie demanding kitchen etiquette from blind Dennis
jokes.explanation: Perfect collision of callbacks - Charlie's chef obsession and Dennis's predicted blindness. Great escalation of both running elements.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Goddamn it, Dennis. You have to yell "corner!" I can't see.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Goddamn it! I think I overdosed on the peppers.

jokes.text: I swear there were other people there. I was strapped into a gurney. I passed out. Uh, they must have put me in an EMT uniform and-and-and fled the scene.
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated story that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Mac's desperate lie is brilliantly absurd - claiming to be victim while clearly being perpetrator. Good commitment to obvious falsehood.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I swear there were other people there. I was strapped into a gurney. I passed out. Uh, they must have put me in an EMT uniform and-and-and fled the scene.
